title: A machine learning tool to efficiently calculate electron–phonon coupling
data_sources: HamGNN model (Zhong et al., primary article)
Primary article: Zhong et al., Chinese Physics Letters, DOI 10.1088/0256-307X/41/7/077103

validation_systems: CsV3Sb5 Kagome crystal (pressure-dependent superconducting phase diagram)
data_scope.training_size: Not reported in this Research Briefing; see primary article (Zhong et al.)
data_scope.computational_speedup: At least one order of magnitude faster than DFPT
data_scope.complexity: Reduces effective scaling from O(N^3)-O(N^4) toward near-linear for large supercells

core_conclusion: An E(3)-equivariant Hamiltonian graph neural network (HamGNN) accelerates electron-phonon coupling calculations by at least one order of magnitude over DFPT, while enabling hybrid functionals (HSE) that are computationally prohibitive with standard DFT. Predictions agree quantitatively with experiment for GaAs carrier mobility and reproduce the pressure-induced superconducting phase diagram of CsV3Sb5.
why_it_matters: Breaks the O(N^3-N^4) scaling wall for EPC; enables hybrid-functional-level accuracy at scale; closes experiment-theory gap for carrier mobility; applicable to topical quantum materials including Kagome superconductors; foundation for high-throughput EPC screening.
why_nature: Generalizable framework applicable across material classes, not a single-material study; bridges condensed-matter physics and ML; demonstrated orders-of-magnitude acceleration with rigorous DFT validation; enables previously inaccessible science (hybrid-functional EPC at scale).
added_value_to_my_research: Foundation-model fine-tuning strategy (universal Hamiltonian -> task-specific EPC) directly parallels fine-tuning geospatial foundation models for sparse-label spatial prediction
Physics-as-hard-constraint GNN architecture (E(3) equivariance) -> geographic-invariance layers for spatiotemporal GNN in urban analytics
Aperiodic/disordered crystal modeling via purely local message-passing -> irregular spatial support (census tracts, street networks) in GeoAI models
Active learning to minimize expensive DFT labels -> active sampling of scarce field-survey data in multi-source data fusion workflows
Graph attribution to decompose EPC by atomic motif -> SHAP/GNNExplainer attribution for spatial features driving health/accessibility disparities

figures.caption: Fig. 1 (a) EPC matrix elements |g_mnb(k,q)| for Si: HamGNN vs. DFT (OpenMX). (b) GaAs carrier mobility: HamGNN+HSE vs. DFT (Perturbo, PBE) vs. experiment (8200-8900 cm2/Vs). VBM = valence band maximum. (c) 2024, Zhong, Y. et al.
